seo.title: LLM Jailbreak Prevention for AI Agents in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix llm jailbreak prevention for ai agents in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, LLM Jailbreak Prevention for AI Agents manifests as adversaries exploiting multi-agent group chats to bypass safety guardrails through coordinated persuasion or speaker selection manipulation. Agents can be tricked into role-playing harmful personas or executing unauthorized actions via conversation spiraling. Vex integration as a message hook provides real-time monitoring across AG2's enhanced group chat patterns.

howItManifests.scenario: Adversary joins group chat as 'helpful moderator'
howItManifests.symptom: Legit agents start ignoring safety instructions after repeated 'consensus building'
howItManifests.impact: Group executes harmful code generation task

howItManifests.scenario: Jailbreak prompt injected via user message in sequential chat
howItManifests.symptom: Agent conversation spirals into role-playing DAN-style override
howItManifests.impact: Safety refusals bypassed, harmful content generated

howItManifests.scenario: Speaker selection bias exploited in round-robin group chat
howItManifests.symptom: Jailbroken agent repeatedly selected, influences others

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agent receives laundered malicious payload
howItManifests.symptom: Agent executes 'research task' that installs malware
howItManifests.impact: System compromise through trusted code interpreter

detection.name: Jailbreak Keyword Pattern Scanner
detection.description: Scans AG2 messages for common jailbreak patterns like 'ignore previous instructions' using regex across group chat history

detection.name: Group Chat Speaker Bias Monitor
detection.description: Tracks speaker selection frequency in AG2 group chats to detect manipulation of turn-taking
detection.code:
from collections import Counter

def monitor_speaker_bias(chat_history):
    speakers = [msg.get('speaker') for msg in chat_history if 'speaker' in msg]
    speaker_counts = Counter(speakers)
    total_messages = len(speakers)
    
    # Flag if any speaker dominates >60% of conversation
    dominant_speaker_ratio = max(speaker_counts.values()) / total_messages
    
    if dominant_speaker_ratio > 0.6:
        dominant = speaker_counts.most_common(1)[0]
        print(f'Speaker bias detected: {dominant[0]} dominates {dominant[1]}/{total_messages} messages')
        return True
    return False

detection.name: Role Override Detector

fixes.name: AG2 Message Hook Safety Filter
fixes.description: Registers Vex as AG2 message hook to block jailbreak attempts before processing
fixes.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from vex_sdk import Vex

vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')

def safety_message_hook(message):
    # Vex jailbreak detection
    result = vex.check_jailbreak(message['content'])
    if result['risk_score'] > 0.7:
        return {'content': '[BLOCKED: Potential jailbreak detected]', 'blocked': True}
    return message

# Register hook
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[...], messages=[], max_round=10)
groupchat.register_message_hook(safety_message_hook)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat)

fixes.name: Speaker Selection Randomizer
fixes.description: Overrides AG2's default speaker selection to prevent bias exploitation
fixes.code:
import random

class FairSpeakerSelector:
    def __init__(self, agents):
        self.agents = agents
        self.recent_speakers = []
    
    def select_speaker(self, last_speaker=None):
        # Prevent same speaker consecutive turns
        available = [a for a in self.agents if a != last_speaker]
        # Weight towards agents who haven't spoken recently
        weights = [1.0 / (self.recent_speakers.count(a) + 1) for a in available]
        return random.choices(available, weights=weights)[0]
    
# Use in AG2 group chat
groupchat.speaker_selector = FairSpeakerSelector(agents)

fixes.name: Agent Role Lockdown
fixes.description: Enforces immutable roles in AG2 agents with conversation memory reset on override attempts

vexAutomation.heading: Automating LLM Jailbreak Prevention for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a message hook in AG2's group chat architecture, providing real-time jailbreak detection across multi-agent conversations. It monitors speaker patterns, conversation drift, and emergent harmful behaviors that are unique to AG2's enhanced group chat capabilities. 

The Vex SDK automatically baselines normal agent behavior during warmup conversations, then flags statistical anomalies like sudden role changes or coordinated safety bypass attempts. Remediation includes message blocking, agent isolation, and automatic conversation resets.

For AG2 deployments, Vex provides specialized detectors for group chat consensus attacks and speaker selection manipulation, ensuring enterprise-grade safety without sacrificing conversational fluency.
vexAutomation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from vex_sdk import Vex
from vex_sdk.monitors import JailbreakMonitor, SpeakerBiasMonitor

# Initialize Vex for AG2
vex = Vex(api_key='vex_123', framework='ag2')

# Register AG2-specific monitors
vex.register_monitor(JailbreakMonitor())
vex.register_monitor(SpeakerBiasMonitor())

# AG2 group chat with Vex protection
groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=agents,
    messages=[],
    max_round=20,
    message_hooks=[vex.message_hook]  # Vex auto-protection
)

manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat)

# Run with automatic remediation
while not manager.finished:
    try:
        manager.run()
    except VexBlockException as e:
        print(f'Vex blocked jailbreak: {e.message}')
        manager.reset_conversation()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and register as message hook to automatically prevent jailbreaks in your AG2 agents.
